notes: - Two of [Math 1A or Math 16A], [Math 1B or Math 16B], Math 53, Math 54, Math 55, or Stat 2. Only one of 1A or 16A and one of 1B or 16B can be used for the major, if taken. Required for admission. - Biology 1A and 1AL. Required for admission. - Biology 1B. Required for admission. - Chem 1A and 1AL. Strongly Recommended for admission, required for graduation. - Physics 8A or 7A. Strongly Recommended for admission, required for graduation. Retain all syllabi (including which reference materials and textbooks were used, methods of assessment, learning objectives, and lecture/topic schedules). Unarticulated courses and series must be reviewed by the respective Berkeley department – a course description is insufficient for review. Students must initiate the review process by contacting Integrative Biology academic advisors after admission. A 2.0 combined major GPA is required. All major requirements must be taken for a letter grade. Split series articulations not taken on the same campus are not accepted, even from within the same system. Split series must be reviewed independently. Students are encouraged to take INTEGBI 77A or 77B their first semester for an introduction to the department’s faculty, research, and courses. It’s also a good opportunity to meet students with similar interests and build community.
Exam credit is not accepted in lieu of required or recommended courses for this major.

Integrative Biology spans hierarchical levels from molecules to ecosystems, incorporating many diverse disciplines to unravel the complexity of biology. The department embraces discovery-based approaches to student learning, encouraging independent research and scientific community engagement. Students in Integrative Biology choose specialized upper division courses, with help from academic advisors, that match their interests. Course variety includes field-based organismal biology and evolution, quantitative and population genetics and genomics, animal behavior, paleontology, anatomy, and physiology. Integrative Biology’s focus on comparative biology and evolution provides a lens for understanding our interconnected living world. For more information about the full major requirements, sample plans, and student opportunities, visit us at: Web: https://ib.berkeley.edu/undergrad Email: ibusso@berkeley.edu Office: 3060 Valley Life Sciences Building Phone: 510-643-8895

COLLEGE ADMISSIONS REQUIREMENTS FOR TRANSFER STUDENTS THIS MAJOR IS OFFERED BY THE COLLEGE OF LETTERS AND SCIENCE (L&S) IMPORTANT information for students applying for admission to L&S for the fall 2025 application cycle and beyond Completion of Essential Skills requirements (Reading & Composition, Quantitative Reasoning, and Foreign Language) or Cal-GETC/IGETC will no longer be a requirement for admission; however, completion of either is strongly recommended prior to enrollment at UC Berkeley to ensure timely degree progress. For more information see the New Policies for Transfer Student Applicants website for L&S: https://ls.berkeley.edu/prospective-students/new-policies-transfer-student-applicants By the end of the spring term preceding fall enrollment at Berkeley, you must complete : 1) The 7-course pattern (as found in the UC Transfer Admission Eligibility Courses Menu in ASSIST): · Two transferable courses in English composition; · One transferable course in mathematical concepts and quantitative reasoning; · Four transferable college courses chosen from at least two of the following subject areas: o arts and humanities o social and behavioral sciences o physical and biological sciences AND 2) Major Requirements : Follow the guidance on preparation for this major below. For more information on admission to UC Berkeley : http://admissions.berkeley.edu For specific information on transfer admission to UC Berkeley: https://admissions.berkeley.edu/apply-to-berkeley/transfer-students/transfer-requirements/ For more information on majors at UC Berkeley : Berkeley Academic Guide: http://guide.berkeley.edu
